---
title: "What AI tools did I use"
output: html_document
---


## ChatGPT

### Prompt 1
In order to get code using the mcp() package I queried ChatGPT

with the prompt:


'Using the R package mcp create a linear model with one breakpoint. The first model is beta_1 x The second model is beta_1 * tau + beta_2 * (x - tau) we want to estimate tau'

It provided some sample code that I copied into RStudio and adapted for our purposes.

### Prompt 2

To fix a bug I used the prompt:

'model <- list(
  strain ~ 0 + stress,        # Segment 1: beta_1 * x (no intercept)
  ~ 0 + I(stress - cp_1) # Segment 2: beta_2 * (x - tau)
)

prior <- list(
  cp_1 ~ dunif(min(aluminium_data$strain), max(aluminium_data$strain))
)

fit <- mcp(model, data = aluminium_data, prior = prior)'

It told me I was missing the response variable in the second formula.

It turns out this was classic wrong answer from AI.


### Prompt 3

can i get this prior <- list( int_1 ~ dnorm(0, 10), strain_1 ~ dnorm(0, 10), strain_2 ~ dnorm(0, 10), cp_1 ~ dunif(min(aluminium_data$strain), max(aluminium_data$strain)), sigma ~ dunif(0, 100) ) with hierarcichal priors
